# The Evolving Dark Energy Landscape: From DESI to Modified Gravity

The nature of dark energy remains the deepest puzzle in modern cosmology. DESI's first-year baryon acoustic oscillation measurements (card-desi-bao-results) have injected fresh urgency into the field by revealing a tantalizing 2.5σ preference for evolving dark energy over the cosmological constant. When combined with CMB and supernova data, the w0-wa parametrization (card-dark-energy-w0wa) yields best-fit values that deviate significantly from w = -1, suggesting the dark energy equation of state may have evolved over cosmic time.

The Hubble tension (card-hubble-tension) adds another dimension to this puzzle. The persistent 4–6σ discrepancy between local and early-universe measurements of H₀ refuses to yield to improved systematics. JWST observations have confirmed the HST Cepheid distance ladder, effectively ruling out the most commonly invoked systematic explanations. If the tension is real, it may point to the same new physics that DESI hints at.

Modified gravity theories (card-modified-gravity) offer a compelling alternative framework. Rather than introducing a new dark energy component, these theories modify general relativity on cosmological scales. The Hubble tension and the DESI dark energy signal could both be manifestations of gravitational physics beyond Einstein's theory. Stage-IV surveys will test GR at the percent level, potentially distinguishing between dark energy and modified gravity.

Perhaps most intriguingly, the DESI data hints at phantom divide crossing (card-phantom-crossing) — the dark energy equation of state passing through w = -1. If confirmed, this would rule out all single minimally-coupled scalar field models, dramatically narrowing the theoretical landscape and pointing toward either multi-field dark energy or an effective description of modified gravity.

---

## Confidence Notes

STRONG: Dark energy w0-wa constraints (conf=4), Hubble tension measurements (conf=4)
EMERGING: DESI BAO results (conf=3), Modified gravity framework (conf=2)
THIN: Phantom crossing evidence (conf=1)
